Patch needs to be rebased.

Patch Set 5: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Some questions:
1) is there already a test case for remounting a target (MDT and OST) if the MGS is down?
2) is this the last code using fsfilt, and can it be removed now?
3) is there some way to defer writing the backup if it is identical to the previous llog? That has caused mount slowness on very full filesystems when the config llog write is looking for free blocks for the backup. It would be better to skip this write in the common case when it is not needed. Possibly the old llogid and record count in the header could be used to check if the backup and the new llog are the same?

The (2) and (3) items are for later patches, but (1) would be better to address in this patch, if it isn't already.

Patch Set 16: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Is there any way to test this code?

Patch Set 16:

If I remember correctly, there are few tests in conf-sanity which rely on backup configuration (like starting OSTs before MDS/MGS).

Patch Set 16: (4 inline comments)

So I did a quick review of this patch and the one thing that really stuck out to me was the handling of  llog_get_context.  It seems this function can return NULL or possibly other error values.

LASSERTing if it can't find the context seems a very rigid approach.  I see that this is used in other existing code spots buy why not just check the error condition and handle the error without the use of LASSERT. 

There is also a spot were we don't do the LASSERT check on the call so at least the approach is inconsistent.

Patch Set 16: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

LU-3286 is a duplicate of this bug, and it can't pass with this fix.

You may also verify in a simpler way:
1. set system up
2. umount mgs
3. umount ostx
4. mount ostx, and it should succeed with local copy llog.

Ldiskfs can pass this test, but zfs not yet.

Patch Set 16:

The culprit might be in mgc_process_cfg_log() mgc_request.c Line 1696:

        if (lctxt && lsi && IS_SERVER(lsi) && !IS_MGS(lsi) &&
            cli->cl_mgc_configs_dir != NULL &&
            lu2dt_dev(cli->cl_mgc_configs_dir->do_lu.lo_dev) ==
            lsi->lsi_dt_dev) {

Patch Set 16:

Keith for llog_get_context you will never get a -EXXX type error, only a NULL will be returned. llog_get_context calls llog_group_get_ctxt then calls llog_ctxt_get if the context exist in the array of ctxts.llog_ctxt_get() does just a atomic increment of the reference count of the ctxt found.
I agree we need to handle the NULL more gracfully then doing
a lassert.

Patch Set 16:

Okay look at the rest of the lustre tree I see the same behavior for handling the llog_context. Lots of lasserts instead of handling the error gracefully.

Patch Set 16:

The llog_get_context() is called always after llog_setup() call and must exists, it can't be just NULL that means that core code was modified wrongly breaking its logic. That is why there are assertions, they can happens only due to wrong code change during development, at least that should be so.

Patch Set 16:

Lai, this particular bug is not about to fix ZFS but just remove lvfs part. If it doesn't make ZFS behavior worse than I see no reason to deny patch. ZFS needs just more work. Does ZFS pass tests you refer to without this patch?

Patch Set 16: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

I'm okay with it, and I'll check why ZFS still fails later.
Patch Set 16: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

parents.subject: LU-2145 server: use unified request handler for MGS

commit.subject: LU-2059 llog: MGC to use OSD API for backup logs
commit.message:
LU-2059 llog: MGC to use OSD API for backup logs

MGC uses lvfs API to access local llogs blocking removal of old code

- MGS is converted to use OSD API for local llogs
- llog_is_empty() and llog_backup() are introduced
- initial OSD start initialize run ctxt so llog can use it too
- shrink dcache after initial scrub in osd-ldiskfs to avoid holding
  data of unlinked objects until umount.

Signed-off-by: Mikhail Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I137e38abb0e93c12406c6610c0922f4294e67290
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5049
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
